name: APT28 Fancy Bear — Long-Term Espionage Campaign
description: A multi-stage, long-term espionage campaign modeled on APT28 (Fancy Bear) operations. The attack spans initial access via spear phishing with a malicious Word document, implant deployment (X-Agent/Sofacy), LSASS credential dumping, domain reconnaissance, lateral movement to sensitive servers, long-term C2 via DNS tunneling (using GoSNRATT), and stealthy data exfiltration via encrypted HTTPS beaconing to CDN-fronted C2 infrastructure.

phases.name: Spear Phishing with Malicious Word Document (Macro)
phases.description: APT28 sends a spear phishing email with a Word document containing a malicious VBA macro that executes a PowerShell stager. The document impersonates a NATO briefing document targeting a defense contractor executive.

indicators.subject: RESTRICTED: NATO Exercise Trident Juncture 2024 Briefing

phases.name: Macro Execution — PowerShell Dropper
phases.description: The VBA macro executes a PowerShell command that downloads and executes the X-Agent (Sofacy) implant from a CDN-fronted C2 server. The implant is injected into explorer.exe via process hollowing.

phases.name: Persistence via Registry Run Key
phases.description: X-Agent implant installs itself in the registry Run key (HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run) and schedules a task disguised as a Windows Update component for resilience.
phases.technique_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys

phases.name: Credential Access — LSASS Dump
phases.description: X-Agent uses its built-in credential harvesting module to dump LSASS memory via a custom kernel driver that bypasses EDR. Credentials are encrypted and staged for exfiltration.

phases.name: DNS Tunneling C2 Communication
phases.description: X-Agent communicates with C2 infrastructure via DNS TXT record queries, encoding data in subdomain labels. Beaconing interval is randomized between 15-45 minutes with jitter to evade behavioral detection.

commands: DNS TXT queries with base64-encoded payload in subdomain labels

phases.name: Lateral Movement to Sensitive Research Server
phases.description: Using harvested domain admin credentials, the attacker moves laterally to the R&D file server and the executive SharePoint instance using PtH (Pass-the-Hash) with the NTLM hash.

phases.name: Long-Term Data Collection and Exfiltration
phases.description: Over several weeks, the implant silently exfiltrates documents, emails, and keylogger output. Data is compressed, encrypted with RSA-2048, and exfiltrated in small chunks disguised as HTTPS telemetry traffic to a CDN-fronted C2.
